description: C26B-024 Step 1 (wη Round 11) perf regression gate baseline. Pins the Native/JS real-time ratio and the Native sys/real ratio for the router bench fixture (examples/quality/c26_native_router_bench/router.td). Intended target = wε Round 10 acceptance (Native/JS <= 2.0, sys/real <= 0.30 on local 16T). The CI 2C thresholds include headroom for noise + reduced parallelism; see .github/workflows/perf-router-baseline.md for the derivation.
sampling_policy: The first min_samples_required successful runs on main push accumulate into sample_count. While sample_count < min_samples_required, threshold violations are WARN-only (no hard-fail). Once sample_count >= min_samples_required, violations hard-fail the job. Manual override: set `strict: true` to force hard-fail immediately; set `strict: false` to keep WARN-only regardless of sample_count.
fixture_scale: N=50 + N=200 routes, M=500 iter, 6 scenarios (best/worst/miss × 2 sizes)

native_js_ratio_max.rationale: wε Round 10 local 16T measured Native/JS = 2.0 (acceptance bar). CI 2C has less parallelism, more malloc contention on a shared glibc arena, and node startup variance; 1.5× headroom keeps the gate informative without false positives. If 5+ CI samples land consistently below 2.5, tighten to 2.5.

sys_real_ratio_max.rationale: wε Round 10 local 16T measured sys/real = 0.09. CI kernels have higher syscall overhead + runner-level tracing, and shorter absolute wall times make sys/real noisier. The published C26B-024 Step 4 acceptance bar is 0.30 (30%); 0.40 gives 1.33× headroom above that so the gate catches regressions approaching 50% (the prior wT Round 8 waypoint) while tolerating CI noise.

notes: Populated by the perf-router.yml workflow on successful runs.
strict=null means: enforce once sample_count >= min_samples_required.
Set strict=true to force hard-fail before sample accumulation.
Set strict=false to keep WARN-only even after the threshold.
